tool.name: Discriminant analysis of principal components (DAPC) to genotype data
tool.description: using Kmeans or a k-fixed (SNPs and SSRs).
tool.command: mkdir -p outputs && Rscript '$__tool_directory__/dapc_kmeans.R' --input '$input_data' --format '$input_format' #if str($k_mode.k_mode_sel) == 'fixed': --k-fixed '$k_mode.k_fixed' #else: --k-min '$k_mode.k_min' --k-max '$k_mode.k_max' --n-start '$k_mode.n_start' --n-runs '$k_mode.n_runs' #end if --n-pca-max '$n_pca_max' #if str($seed_mode.seed_mode_sel) == 'random': --seed 'NULL' #else if str($seed_mode.seed_mode_sel) == 'custom': --seed '$seed_mode.seed_value' #else: --seed '42' #end if

param.name: k_mode_sel
param.label: Method for selecting the number of clusters (K)

param.name: k_min
param.label: Minimum K
param.help: Lower limit of the range of K tested.

param.name: k_max
param.label: K maximum (mode auto)
param.help: Upper limit. Increase if the selected K = K_max.

param.name: n_start
param.label: Number of K-means iterations (nstart)
param.help: Number of random initialisations for K-means. Increasing this value improves stability at the expense of computation time.

param.name: n_runs
param.label: Number of find.clusters() repetitions (n-runs)
param.help: Number of independent find.clusters() runs used to compute the confidence interval on the BIC curve. Each run uses a different random seed. More runs produce a more reliable interval but increase computation time.

param.name: k_fixed
param.label: Select the value of K
param.help: Number of clusters to be used for DAPC.

param.name: n_pca_max
param.label: Maximum number of PCA axes
param.help: Maximum number of PCA axes supplied to K-means.

tool.help:
Usage =====
This tool performs a Discriminant Analysis of Principal Components (DAPC) to identify genetic clusters using the R package adegenet (Jombart *et al*. 2010). It is designed to work with SNPs (VCF format) or SSRs (tabular format).
The analysis follows a process in three steps:
**1. Principal Component Analysis**
PCA first reduces the data to a tractable set of orthogonal axes summarising the main axes of genetic variation. For K effective populations, only the first K-1 PC axes carry biologically meaningful structure (Patterson *et al*., 2006).
**2. Clustering**
Groups are identified using K-means clustering applied to the PCA scores, as implemented in the *find.clusters* function of the adegenet package (Jombart, 2008; Jombart & Ahmed, 2011). Two modes are available:
- *Automatic selection*: K-means is run for a user-defined range of K values (from K-min to K-max). The optimal number of clusters corresponds to the K value with the lowest the Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC), which penalises model complexity and avoids overfitting (Jombart *et al*., 2010). The retained K corresponds to the "elbow" of the BIC curve. To quantify this variability, *find.clusters()* is run **n-runs** independent times, each with a distinct random seed (seed, seed+1, seed+2, …). Every run internally performs **nstart** random initialisations and retains the best one, so the BIC value recorded per run already represents the best solution found across those initialisations. The K retained for downstream analysis is the one with the lowest BIC across all runs and all initialisations (i.e. the global best solution).
- *Fixed selection*: The user defines a number of clusters directly, bypassing the BIC inference step.
A random seed can be set to ensure the reproducibility of K-means stochastic initialisation.
**3. Discriminant Analysis of Principal Components (DAPC)**
DAPC (Jombart *et al*., 2010) maximises between-group relative to within-group genetic variance, providing sharper resolution of population structure than PCA alone. To avoid overfitting, the number of PC axes fed to the discriminant analysis is capped at K-1 following Thia (2023), with K determined by K-means or defined by the user, depending on the user’s choice.
The tool generates the following outputs:
- Summary statistics file (label: Summary stats) - Individual coordinates on the DAPC discriminant axes (label: LD scores) - Assignment table including K-means cluster, DAPC-assigned cluster, K-means/DAPC concordance, and posterior membership probabilities for each cluster (label: Assignments) - BIC curve (automatic mode only) with confidence interval. - PCA scree plot showing the variance explained by each axis, with cumulative variance overlaid - PCA and DAPC scatter plots - Posterior membership barplot showing the assignment probability of each individual
Example files =============
**SSR Genotype file (tab):**
::
Ind Pop Loc1 Loc2 Loc3 ind1 pop1 120/124 200/202 150/150 ind2 pop1 120/120 202/204 150/152 ind3 pop2 124/126 200/200 152/152
Note : The Pop column must be included, but it may contain 'N/A' entries.
**LD scores files:**
+------------------------+----------------+---------+---------+ | Individual | Cluster_kmeans | LD1 | LD2 | +========================+================+=========+=========+ | GA-2_2021_A11-01-m21_1 | 1 | -2.0618 | 1.4627 | +------------------------+----------------+---------+---------+ | GA-2_2022_A11-01-m22_1 | 2 | -0.9416 | -1.5315 | +------------------------+----------------+---------+---------+ | GA-2_2021_A11-02-m21_1 | 2 | -3.6633 | -0.4452 | +------------------------+----------------+---------+---------+ | ... | ... | ... | ... | +------------------------+----------------+---------+---------+
Note : The number of LD columns equals K-1.
**Assignments file:**
+-------------------------+-------------------+-----------------+---------------------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+---------+---------+---------+ | Individual | K_selected_kmeans | K_selected_dapc | Cluster_assignment_kmeans | Cluster_assignment_dapc | Kmeans_DAPC_concordance | Prob_K1 | Prob_K2 | Prob_K3 | +=========================+===================+=================+===========================+=========================+=========================+=========+=========+=========+ | GA-2_2021_A11-01-m21_1 | 3 | 3 | 2 | 2 | TRUE | 0.9483 | 0.0501 | 0.0016 | +-------------------------+-------------------+-----------------+---------------------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+---------+---------+---------+ | GA-2_2022_A11-01-m22_1 | 3 | 3 | 1 | 1 | TRUE | 0.0648 | 0.9080 | 0.0271 | +-------------------------+-------------------+-----------------+---------------------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+---------+---------+---------+ | GA-2_2021_A11-04-m21_1 | 3 | 3 | 2 | 3 | FALSE | 0.2206 | 0.1802 | 0.5992 | +-------------------------+-------------------+-----------------+---------------------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+---------+---------+---------+ | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | +-------------------------+-------------------+-----------------+---------------------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+---------+---------+---------+
Notes : - Kmeans_DAPC_concordance indicates whether K-means and DAPC assignments agree. - Prob_K1, Prob_K2, ... sum to 1 per row. The number of Prob_K columns equals K.
